senses.definition: A medical procedure to remove cerebrospinal fluid from the spinal canal for diagnosis or treatment.
examples: Rachicentesis is performed to test the CSF for infections.
The doctors discussed performing rachicentesis to assess pressure.

senses.definition: Plural form of rachis; the main axis or spine-like structure in plants or animals.
examples: The rachides of some inflorescences are long and slender.
In botany, rachides support the leaflets along the stem.

senses.definition: Relating to or situated on the rachis (the main axis, spine).
examples: Rachidial structures were observed along the plant's central axis.
The rachidial nerves run near the spinal column.

senses.definition: Relating to the rachis; spinal or vertebral.
examples: A rachidian feature was noted in the vertebral column.
Rachidian nerves emerge from the spinal cord.

senses.definition: Having a shape or form like a rachis; spine-like.
examples: The plant exhibited a rachiform stem with a central axis.
Rachiform projections were noted along the organ's length.

senses.definition: Anatomical term for the ridge or tongue-like structure on a rachis or similar part.
examples: The rachiglossa is a small dorsal ridge near the spine.
Researchers described a prominent rachiglossa in the specimen.

senses.definition: Having a tongue-like projection or structure associated with a rachis.
examples: The fossil showed rachiglossate features along the dorsal axis.
Rachiglossate appendages were observed in the arthropod specimen.

senses.definition: A device or instrument used to record measurements along a rachis or spine-like axis.
examples: The researcher used a rachigraph to map the spine's curvature.
A rachigraph recorded the growth along the axis.

senses.definition: A small rachis; a tiny axis in certain plant structures that bears items like seeds or leaflets.
examples: The rachilla bears the tiny seeds.
In some grasses, the rachilla is a short segment between parts.

senses.definition: Plural of rachilla; small axes bearing seeds or leaflet components.
examples: The rachillae carry the seeds in some cereals.
Observing rachillae helps understand seed arrangement.

senses.definition: Another plural form of rachilla; small axes in botanical structures.
examples: The rachillas are short and bear seeds.
Different species have varying numbers of rachillas.

senses.definition: A medical procedure to withdraw cerebrospinal fluid from the spinal canal for testing or treatment.
examples: Rachiocentesis is used to diagnose infections or bleeding in the CNS.
The patient underwent rachiocentesis after the abnormal CSF results.

senses.definition: To anesthetize a region near the spine (often by injecting anesthetic near the spinal cord).
examples: The surgeon planned to rachiococainize the lower back before the procedure.
An anesthetic was used to rachiococainize the operative area.

senses.definition: A spinal deformity involving a forward bending of the spine (kyphosis localized to the spine).
examples: Radiographs showed rachiocyphosis in the thoracic region.
Management of ra chiocyphosis may include bracing or surgery.

senses.definition: An organism or part bearing a spine-like tooth or tooth-like projection.
examples: The fossil showed rachiodont features with spine-like dentition.
In some extinct species, rachiodont structures were prominent.

senses.definition: Pain located along the spine; spinal pain.
examples: The patient reported ra chiodynia after the injury.
Rachiodynia can be caused by nerve irritation.

senses.definition: A device used to measure properties along the spine or axis of a structure.
examples: The ra chiometer recorded spinal curvature precisely.
Researchers used a ra chiometer during the study.

senses.definition: Inflammation of the spinal cord and surrounding membranes.
examples: Rachiomyelitis requires urgent medical treatment.
Symptoms include neurological deficits from ra chiomyelitis.

senses.definition: Paralysis involving the spinal cord or nerves along the spine.
examples: Rachioparalysis can result from nerve injury.
Treatment focuses on rehabilitation after ra chioparalysis.

senses.definition: Paralysis of the spinal region or related nerves.
examples: The patient recovered from ra chioplegia with therapy.
Ra chioplegia may involve loss of motor function in the limbs.

senses.definition: A curvature of the spine involving the thoracic region.
examples: X-rays showed ra chi oscoliosis requiring monitoring.
Treatment options for rachioscoliosis include bracing and surgery.

senses.definition: A surgical instrument used to cut or puncture along the spine or spinal area.
examples: The surgeon used a rachio­tome to access the spinal canal.
Rachio­tomes are designed for delicate spine procedures.

senses.definition: Surgical procedures involving cutting the spine or spinal structures; multiple such surgeries.
examples: The team performed several rachitomies during the operation.
Rachitomies require careful planning and imaging beforehand.

senses.definition: A surgical cutting of the spine or spinal structures; a spinal incision.
examples: Rachotomy is a specialized procedure in neurosurgery.
The patient recovered after a carefully planned rachotomy.

senses.definition: A rare congenital condition where two individuals are joined at the spine (spinal) region.
examples: Rachipagus twins are joined at the spine.
Surgical separation of rachipagus twins is complex and high risk.

senses.definition: Surgical procedures involving cutting into the spine; plural of rachotomy.
examples: Several ra chiotomies were performed to relieve pressure.
Rachiotomy procedures require detailed preoperative planning.

senses.definition: A surgical operation involving cutting into the spine.
examples: The ra chiotomy aimed to relieve nerve compression.
Postoperative care followed the ra chiotomy.

senses.definition: A rare form of conjoined twins joined at the spine.
examples: Rachiopagus twins share a spinal connection.
Medical teams assess the feasibility of separation in rach I opagus cases.

senses.definition: An abnormal side-to-side curvature of the spine with thoracic involvement.
examples: Rachioscoliosis can affect posture and breathing.
Treatment options include bracing or surgery for rachioscoliosis.

senses.definition: Duplicate entry note: see rachio­tome.
A surgical operation involving cutting into the spine.
examples: The surgeon performed a rachotomy to access the spinal canal.
Rachotomy requires careful imaging before the procedure.

senses.definition: Conjoined twins joined at the spine; a rare form of conjoined twins.
examples: Rachipagus twins require multidisciplinary planning for separation.
Prenatal imaging can detect rachipagus conditions.
